stories.title: Anthropic-Pentagon battle shows how big tech has reversed course on AI and war
stories.description: Less than a decade ago, Google employees scuttled any military use of its AI. Now Anthropic is fighting Trump officials not over if, but how  The standoff between Anthropic and the Pentagon has forced the tech industry to once again grapple with the question of how its products are used for war – a

stories.title: Palantir Demos Show How the Military Could Use AI Chatbots to Generate War Plans
stories.description: Software demos and Pentagon records detail how chatbots like Anthropic’s Claude could help the Pentagon analyze intelligence and suggest next steps.

stories.title: ‘Invasive’ AI-led mass surveillance in Africa violating freedoms, warn experts
stories.description: Countries across the continent have spent more than $2bn on Chinese tracking technology that is not ‘necessary or proportionate’, new report finds  The rapid expansion of AI-powered mass-surveillance systems across Africa is violating citizens’ right to privacy and having a chilling effect on socie

stories.title: Binance sues WSJ, panicked by gov’t probes into sanctioned crypto transfers
stories.description: Binance’s lawsuit accusing WSJ of defamation unlikely to stall government probes.

stories.title: Pentagon Report: U.S. Military Fired Missile at Elementary School in Iran
stories.description:
Despite attempts by Trump to claim otherwise, the U.S. military was responsible for killing at least 175 in a strike on a school in Iran. 
 The post  Pentagon Report: U.S. Military Fired Missile at Elementary School in Iran  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: Government Spying 🤝 Targeted Advertising | EFFector 38.5
stories.description: Have you ever seen a really creepy targeted ad online? One that revealed just how much these companies know about your life? It's unsettling enough to see how much companies know about you—but now we have confirmation that the government is also tapping the advertising surveillance machine to ge

stories.title: From Flock to ICE, Here’s a Breakdown of How You’re Being Watched
stories.description: To better understand what exactly we’re looking at in this dystopian surveillance hellscape, 404 Media’s Jason Koebler and Joseph Cox joined Reddit's r/technology for an Ask Me Anything session.

stories.title: Amazon is determined to use AI for everything – even when it slows down work
stories.description: Corporate employees said Amazon’s race to roll out AI is leading to surveillance, slop and ‘more work for everyone’.  When Dina, a software developer based in New York, joined Amazon two years ago, her job was to write code. Now, it’s mostly fixing what artificial intelligence breaks.  The internal

stories.title: Anduril snaps up space surveillance firm ExoAnalytic Solutions
stories.description: The deal could give Anduril a better shot at lucrative missile defense contracts through the "Golden Dome" system.

stories.title: The Government Must Not Force Companies to Participate in AI-powered Surveillance
stories.description: The rapidly escalating conflict between Anthropic and the Pentagon, which started when the company  refused  to let the government use its technology to spy on Americans, has now gone to court. The Department of Defense retaliated by designating the company a “supply chain risk” (SCR). Now, Anth

stories.title: Scientists Discover Vast Ancient Trade Network That Rewrites History with Parrot DNA
stories.description: “I think we often underestimate their capabilities,” said one of the researchers who uncovered  a pre-Inca trade route linking the Amazon rainforest to the Pacific coast.

stories.title: Anthropic sues US over blacklisting; White House calls firm "radical left, woke"
stories.description: Anthropic says it was blacklisted for opposing autonomous weapons, mass surveillance.

stories.title: DHS Ousts CBP Privacy Officers Who Questioned ‘Illegal’ Orders
stories.description: Department of Homeland Security leaders removed top privacy officers who objected to mislabeling government records to block their public release, WIRED has learned.

stories.title: US military contractor likely built iPhone hacking tools used by Russian spies in Ukraine
stories.description: Google found a series of hacking tools they said were used by a Russian espionage group and a cybercriminal group in China. Sources from a U.S. government defense contractor said some of those hacking tools were theirs.

stories.title: With World's Eyes on Iran, Israel Locks Down the West Bank
stories.description:
The Israeli military has closed checkpoints around the West Bank, restricting Palestinians’ movement as settler violence ramps up. 
 The post  With World’s Eyes on Iran, Israel Locks Down the West Bank  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: An iPhone-hacking toolkit used by Russian spies likely came from U.S military contractor
stories.description: Google found a series of hacking tools they said were used by a Russian espionage group and a cybercriminal group in China. Sources from a U.S. government defense contractor said some of those hacking tools were theirs.

stories.title: Islamophobic Think Tank Helped Prosectors Write Terror Indictment Against ICE Protesters
stories.description:
The far-right Center for Security Policy, led by anti-Muslim activist Frank Gaffney, is best known for pedaling conspiracy theories. 
 The post  Islamophobic Think Tank Helped Prosectors Write Terror Indictment Against ICE Protesters  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.description: The    SAFE act,    introduced by Senators Mike Lee (R-UT) and Dick Durbin (D-IL), is the first of many likely proposals we will see to reauthorize Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act (FISA) Amendments Act of 2008 — and while imperfect, it does propose a litany of real and

stories.title: U.S. Military Refuses to Endorse Trump Claim That Iran Bombed Girls’ School
stories.description:
“It would be inappropriate to comment given the incident is under investigation,” CENTCOM told The Intercept. 
 The post  U.S. Military Refuses to Endorse Trump Claim That Iran Bombed Girls’ School  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: OpenAI on Surveillance and Autonomous Killings: You’re Going to Have to Trust Us
stories.description:
OpenAI says Americans shouldn’t worry about the ethics of its new Pentagon contract. You’ll have to take their word for it (and Pete Hegseth’s). 
 The post  OpenAI on Surveillance and Autonomous Killings: You’re Going to Have to Trust Us  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: Columbia Flouted Its Own Policies and Let ICE Into University Buildings
stories.description:
The policy has been in place for at least a year — but school security keeps letting ICE agents in. 
 The post  Columbia Flouted Its Own Policies and Let ICE Into University Buildings  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.description: Tech policy professor who served in US air force explains how a feud between an AI startup and the US military illuminates ethical fault lines  Anthropic’s ongoing fight with the Department of Defense over what  safety restrictions  it can put on its  artificial intelligence  models has captivated

stories.title: Anthropic’s Pentagon deal is a cautionary tale for startups chasing federal contracts
stories.description: The Pentagon has officially designated Anthropic a supply-chain risk after the two failed to agree on how much control the military should have over its AI models, including its use in autonomous weapons and mass domestic surveillance. As Anthropic’s $200 million contract fell apart, the DoD turned

stories.title: ICE Poses a Real Threat to Our Elections
stories.description:
DHS has built a national police force with massive surveillance capabilities — which it could use to interfere with our elections. 
 The post  ICE Poses a Real Threat to Our Elections  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: Weasel Words: OpenAI’s Pentagon Deal Won’t Stop AI‑Powered Surveillance
stories.description: OpenAI, the maker of ChaptGPT, is rightfully facing widespread criticism for its decisions to fill the gap the U.S. Department of Defense (DoD) created when rival Anthropic    refused to    drop its restrictions against using its AI for surveillance and autonomous weapons systems. guardrails for generative AI are deeply lacking, and it has been shown how easily 
compromised they are, intentionally or inadvertently,” Heidy Khlaaf, the chief AI

stories.title: Workers report watching Ray-Ban Meta-shot footage of people using the bathroom
stories.description: Meta accused of "concealing the facts" about smart glass users' privacy.

stories.title: OpenAI Had Banned Military Use. The Pentagon Tested Its Models Through Microsoft Anyway
stories.description: Sources allege the Defense Department experimented with Microsoft’s version of OpenAI technology before the ChatGPT-maker lifted its prohibition on military applications.

stories.title: FBI investigating hack on its wiretap and surveillance systems: Report
stories.description: Hackers allegedly broke into the FBI’s networks, according to a report by CNN.

stories.title: OpenAI introduces GPT-5.4 with more knowledge-work capability
stories.description: Updates come amid user blowback over the company's Pentagon deal.

stories.title: The Controversies Finally Caught Up to Kristi Noem
stories.description: Donald Trump said he would replace the secretary of the Department of Homeland Security. Noem’s tenure was marked by aggressive anti-immigration tactics and ICE and CBP’s killing of two US protesters.

stories.title: Military Leaders See Iran War as “God’s Divine Plan” — a Chilling Turn for Trump’s Fascism
stories.description:
A military watchdog has been “inundated” with complaints that officials are using end-times Christian rhetoric to justify war. 
 The post  Military Leaders See Iran War as “God’s Divine Plan” — a Chilling Turn for Trump’s Fascism  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: Congress Is Considering Abolishing Your Right to Be Anonymous Online
stories.description:
The bipartisan push to remove anonymity from the internet is ushering in an era of unprecedented mass surveillance and censorship. 
 The post  Congress Is Considering Abolishing Your Right to Be Anonymous Online  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: What AI Models for War Actually Look Like
stories.description: While companies like Anthropic debate limits on military uses of AI, Smack Technologies is training models to plan battlefield operations.

stories.description:

stories.title: Podcast: The Depravity Economy
stories.description: How Polymarket and Kalshi bet on Iran; AI translations are impacting Wikipedia; and an Amazon change impacting wishlists.

stories.title: Your Questions Answered: Filming ICE Interactions Safely
stories.description: Despite government attacks against protesters and the press, documenting interactions with law enforcement and federal agents is protected by the First Amendment. ACLU experts break down what you need to know.

stories.title: Letter to the BoPC on Flock Safety
stories.description:
Read our letter to the Board of Police Commissioners exposing LAPD's partnership with Flock Safety and its harms to our communities. Join our fight to shut down Flock!  
 The post  Letter to the BoPC on Flock Safety  appeared first on  Stop LAPD Spying Coalition .

stories.title: The Regime Change President Who Won’t (or Can’t) Actually Change Any Regimes
stories.description:
The Iran war shows that Trump is loving his military interventions — but they are never what he claims them to be. 
 The post  The Regime Change President Who Won’t (or Can’t) Actually Change Any Regimes  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: Amazon Data Centers on Fire After Iranian Missile Strikes on Dubai
stories.description: Some AWS services are down in the Middle East. Recovery is unclear as it requires 'careful assessment to ensure the safety of our operators,' according to Amazon.

stories.description:

stories.title: ICE is Rapidly Expanding Dangerous 287(g) Agreements with Local Police
stories.description: A new ACLU report, “Deputized for Disaster,” illustrates the dangers of the Trump administration’s rapid expansion of the 287(g) program — and how communities are increasingly afraid of local law enforcement officers sworn to protect them.

stories.title: Rümeysa Öztürk: I Saw the Horrors of ICE Detention Firsthand. It is No Place for a Child.
stories.description: As she witnesses the news of ICE detaining thousands of children, child development scholar Rümeysa Öztürk reflects on how detention and deportation negatively impact children for a lifetime, in relation to her own experiences of being detained.

stories.description:

stories.title: “Free” Surveillance Tech Still Comes at a High and Dangerous Cost
